commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d682d08a7b6759eb45bbc8606ae1a86cedea9f6d)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an agent-role evaluation CLI, reusable contract catalog, workflow helper, scheduled/manual GitHub Actions workflow, package scripts, and Vitest coverage.

PR surface: Tests +832, Config +119, Other +1989. Total +2940 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: an agent config that omits `agentDir` will be checked under `~/.openclaw-director-state`, while current runtime resolves the same omitted value under the canonical OpenClaw state dir.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical repair in the new harness plus one focused regression test.

target.title: Add agent role eval harness
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a minimal agent role evaluation harness: CLI, workflow, workflow helper, reusable contract catalog, tests, and package scripts.
- Creates a clean prerequisite for follow-up Browser Steward runtime-enforcement work without carrying the older 49-commit / 300-file stacked branch.
- Intended outcome: maintainers can review and land the reusable harness independently before Browser Steward adds its focused contract/runtime checks.
- Intentionally out of scope: Browser Steward runtime enforcement, unrelated dashboard/music/backup/SNES work, generated bundle hashes, and any credential/session mutation.
- Review focus: harness boundaries, workflow validity, test coverage, and the fact that the diff is limited to the minimal prerequisite surface.

## Linked context

Related: https://github.com/SnowBelt/openclaw/pull/1
Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93018

Requested by: local OpenClaw agent-build audit workflow to unblock Browser Steward upstream integration safely.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Browser Steward runtime-enforcement PR was blocked by an unsafe prerequisite branch containing 49 commits and 300 files. This PR provides the minimal reusable agent-role-eval harness needed as a clean upstream prerequisite.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/agent-role-eval-harness-clean`, based on current `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm agents:eval:contracts`; `node scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs --contracts-only --json`; `pnpm test test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs scripts/agent-role-eval-workflow.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-evals.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-eval-workflow.mjs test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`; `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`; `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-precommit-venv/bin:$PATH pnpm check:workflows`; `env NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build`.
- Evidence after fix: Local terminal output showed the contract catalog passing with 51 contracts and 8 critical contracts, the focused test file passing 24/24 tests, `zizmor` workflow audit passing, and `pnpm build` completing successfully.
- Observed result after fix: The harness validates deterministically, the workflow passes local workflow sanity, and the build succeeds while the branch remains one commit with seven files changed.
- What was not tested: Scheduled GitHub Actions execution and live agent turns in CI were not run from this PR yet.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:workflows` required a temporary `/tmp` Python virtualenv for `pre-commit`; no repo-tracked dependency files were changed for that local tool setup.
- Before evidence: The previous upstream Browser Steward PR targeted `main` directly and showed 47 commits / 100 files, proving the stacked branch was too large for focused review.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm agents:eval:contracts`
- `node scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs --contracts-only --json`
- `pnpm test test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs scripts/agent-role-eval-workflow.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-evals.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-eval-workflow.mjs test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`
- `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-precommit-venv/bin:$PATH pnpm check:workflows`
- `env NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build`

Regression coverage added:
- `test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts` covers contract catalog validity, live prompt scoring, self-contained eval state, workflow input resolution, live-report verification, Ollama bootstrap planning, configured-agent static checks, and failure cases.

Known validation notes:
- First `pnpm check:workflows` failed because local `pre-commit` was missing; reran successfully using a temporary `/tmp` venv.
- First `pnpm build` failed because sandboxing blocked the repo-local heavy-check lock under Git metadata; reran successfully with escalated local filesystem access.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, adds package scripts and a scheduled/manual workflow.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, adds an evaluation workflow that can run live evals when configured; secrets are read only by the live workflow steps and are not logged by this PR.

Highest-risk area: CI workflow and live-eval secret handling.

Mitigation: Workflow defaults run only deterministic contract catalog checks unless scheduled/live settings opt in; local workflow sanity, `zizmor`, focused tests, and build passed.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and CI on this clean prerequisite PR.

Waiting on: upstream CI and maintainer acceptance. Browser Steward upstream PR should wait until this prerequisite lands or is explicitly accepted.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet on this PR.
